mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount factors, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show a positive mean during rate hikes with p-value below 0.05, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real personal income and housing starts indicates collapsing consumer demand and construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with bivariate regression coefficients statistically indistinguishable from zero over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production growth exceeding two percent year-over-year, expanding manufacturing output supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above two percent correlates with negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of observations, or the signal loses statistical significance when filtered for early-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and prompts anticipatory monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL consistently coincides with positive 20-day forward returns, or the correlation coefficient remains above plus 0.10 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in nonfarm payrolls exceeding fifty thousand signals abrupt labor market contraction and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of months following a payroll decline exceeding 50000, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for seasonal adjustments.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and real personal income reflects broad-based economic strength and rising household purchasing power, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined growth signal fails to predict positive returns, with interaction term coefficients statistically insignificant at p greater than 0.10 in quarterly regression models.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production expands while monetary policy remains accommodative, rising factory output translates into higher corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns do not materialize during easing cycles with rising INDPRO, or the predictive power reverses sign when FEDFUNDS is below 2.0 percent.
